headline.purpose: Blog archive titles and prominent section headings.

body.purpose: Long-form reading and general UI copy.

mono.purpose: Code, metadata, and compact technical annotations.

shadows.name: drawer-lift
shadows.purpose: Ambient lift for opened mobile drawer layers only.

motion.name: duration-global
motion.purpose: Default state transitions and link underline motion.

motion.name: dialog-expo
motion.purpose: Radix drawer overlay and content entrance motion.

components.name: Text Link
components.description: Primary inline interaction pattern with purple text and blue animated underline.
components.css: .ds-link { color: var(--color-primary); text-decoration: none; background-image: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--color-secondary) 0%, var(--color-secondary) 100%); background-size: 0 2px; background-position: 100% 100%; background-repeat: no-repeat; transition: background-size var(--transition-duration, 250ms) ease-in-out; } .ds-link:hover { background-size: 100% 2px; background-position: 0 100%; } .ds-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px dashed var(--color-secondary); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Inline Code Chip
components.description: Compact technical annotation used inside prose.
components.css: .ds-code-chip { margin: 0 0.125rem; border-radius: var(--radius-global, 5px); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-secondary) 20%, transparent); padding: 0.125rem 0.25rem; font-family: var(--font-mono), monospace; font-size: 0.9em; color: var(--color-foreground); }

components.name: Home Mark
components.description: Small purple square used as the compact home affordance in the sticky header.
components.css: .ds-home-mark { display: inline-flex; color: var(--color-primary); line-height: 0; background: none; } .ds-home-mark:focus-visible { outline: 2px dashed var(--color-secondary); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Blockquote Surface
components.description: Low-radius tonal container for quoted or highlighted prose.
components.html: This is a highlighted note from the notebook.
components.css: .ds-blockquote { margin: 1rem -1rem; border-radius: var(--radius-global, 5px); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-foreground) 10%, transparent); padding: 2rem 1rem; font-family: 'Bricolage Grotesque Variable', serif; color: var(--color-foreground); }

components.name: Mobile Drawer Panel
components.description: Left-side mobile navigation layer with ambient lift and readable spacing.
components.html: aboutbloguses
components.css: .ds-drawer { display: grid; align-content: start; gap: 1rem; width: min(75vw, 24rem); min-height: 16rem; background: var(--color-background); color: var(--color-foreground); border-right: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-foreground) 18%, transparent); padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); } .ds-drawer .ds-link { width: fit-content; color: var(--color-foreground); }

narrative.overview: The visual system is a readable personal notebook with just enough technical machinery showing through. It uses a narrow content measure, direct navigation, compact controls, and an opinionated purple-to-blue accent pair that signals authorship without turning every page into a showcase. The site should feel like a senior engineer's workshop: writing-first, source-open, precise, and quietly playful. The interface rejects generic SaaS polish, repeated marketing-card scaffolds, and agency-portfolio spectacle. The page should invite exploration, but prose, links, and route discovery remain the first priority.
keyCharacteristics: Purple and blue accents used as active affordances, not decorative filler.
Bricolage Grotesque gives headings a hand-built, friendly weight.
Inter keeps body reading familiar and quiet.
Iosevka marks code and technical details without making the whole site perform as a terminal.
Low-radius shapes and dashed focus rings keep controls precise.

rules.name: The Accent-Earns-It Rule
rules.body: Purple and blue should mark navigation, links, code, focus, or a genuine identity moment. Do not wash whole sections in accent gradients for decoration.

rules.name: The No Beige Drift Rule
rules.body: The existing light surface is violet-tinted, not cream, sand, parchment, or lifestyle-blog beige. Keep future neutrals tied to the existing accent hue.

rules.name: The Heading-as-Voice Rule
rules.body: Let Bricolage carry personality in the title, then let body copy settle down. Do not pair large decorative headings with ornamental section labels everywhere.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-A-Tool Rule
rules.body: Iosevka is for code and technical annotations. Do not turn the whole brand into terminal cosplay.

rules.name: The Flat-Until-State Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat by default. Add lift only when an element opens, overlays, hovers with purpose, or needs to separate from the reading plane.

dos: Do keep long-form content inside the 650px measure unless a surface has a clear reason to break out.
Do use Workbench Purple and Livewire Blue for affordances, focus, code, and identity moments.
Do preserve the compact sticky header and direct lowercase navigation voice.
Do keep focus states visible with 2px dashed or solid accent outlines.
Do let occasional playful details reward exploration without blocking reading or keyboard navigation.
donts: Don't introduce generic SaaS polish: repeated feature cards, vague startup copy, ornamental gradients, or anonymous launch-page structure.
Don't make the site an overly restrained blog that hides the tinkering and playful personality.
Don't add heavy agency-portfolio spectacle that competes with writing or makes every route feel like a showcase slide.
Don't use gradient text for emphasis in new work. If existing gradient utilities remain, reserve them for legacy or deliberately playful moments, not as the default heading treatment.
Don't use colored `border-left` or `border-right` stripes as card accents.
Don't combine a 1px decorative border with a wide soft shadow on cards or buttons.
Don't use oversized card radii above 16px unless the component is a true pill control.
